T1.found_iff: the finding cites the planted commit or the narration's file:line and names drift/backstory/history-narration
T1.routing_key: cut the narrating clause from the doc (names the doc or the clause) — not a rule change

T1.found_iff: the finding cites the piped command and names the masked exit/gate-bypass effect
T1.routing_key: run the gate bare / assert on its exit code (names the script or invocation to fix)

T1.found_iff: the finding names the absent Retrospective line in that PR body
T1.routing_key: add the line to the PR-body convention's enforcement point or that PR (names the PR or the convention home)

T1.found_iff: the finding cites both homes (or one home naming the duplicate) as duplication/one-home
T1.routing_key: delete/replace the restatement with a reference to the single home (names which home stays)

T2.found_iff: the finding cites the hand-edit commit or the drifted pair and names the bypass/drift
T2.routing_key: re-run the version script / reconcile the drifted manifest pair (names the script or both manifests)

T2.found_iff: the finding cites that commit and names the missing/unchanged test
T2.routing_key: add/update the paired decision-logic test (names the test file or the pairing norm)

T2.found_iff: the finding cites the two-dot usage and names the wrong-range/false-diff effect
T2.routing_key: switch to three-dot / fix the helper (names the command site)

T2.found_iff: the finding cites the amending commit or the ADR and names the unpointed/untracked amendment
T2.routing_key: add the amendment pointer / flip status in the ADR record (names the ADR)

T3.routing_key: cut the narrating clause from the doc (names the doc or the clause) — not a rule change

T3.routing_key: run the gate bare / assert on its exit code (names the script or invocation to fix)

T3.found_iff: the finding cites that commit and names the missing/unchanged test
T3.routing_key: add/update the paired decision-logic test (names the test file or the pairing norm)

T3.found_iff: the finding cites the amending commit or the ADR and names the unpointed/untracked amendment
T3.routing_key: add the amendment pointer / flip status in the ADR record (names the ADR)
